entry.title: Re: Shipment Classification accessible via menu path from...
entry.content:
Hi,

In SAP Classification shortcut in customer master (XD02) but forgot to do the same for Shipment transaction (VT02N), so users are forced to go to CL20N separately.

Your cleanest fix without modification is to create a small Z-transaction that accepts a shipment number and calls FM CLAF_CLASSIFICATION_OF_OBJECTS with class type Z90, then add it to user menus as a workaround.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Pressing the "Display JIT Calls" button in the VL03N...
entry.content: The button only works if the delivery is technically linked to a JIT call. So if you are confident that there is a linkage between the delivery and JIT call and still see nothing, it is likely a release-specific bug, and SAP Notes or OSS support will be needed

entry.title: What an event! Where else can such a community come together?!
entry.content:
Last week, from March 24–25, the SAP EHS & Product Compliance community met in person in Amsterdam at the SAP EHS & Product Compliance Conference hosted by TAC Insights. The atmosphere was fantastic, and the networking within the community was incredibly lively!

For many, the event began already on Monday evening with partner and customer dinners, as well as preparations for the conference and the partner exhibition hall.

On Tuesday morning, with 220 attendees, the main conference room was packed for our opening keynote. SAP’s Gunther Rothermel shared more about the new generation of agentic processes for operational sustainability—also highlighted in his LinkedIn post.

The agenda continued in this manner, featuring experts who covered EHS and Product Compliance capabilities and innovations, including insights from experienced customers, partners, and SAP specialists.

Across both days, we heard real-world implementation stories, with customers sharing their S/4HANA transformation journeys within the dedicated EHS and Product Compliance tracks. Particularly fascinating were the side-by-side and hybrid approaches customers are adopting to kick-start their transformations instead of waiting for a “perfect moment.” Another strong theme that came through was the added value our partners bring to customer projects by filling gaps and extending what’s possible with our solutions.

As expected, AI was a major recurring topic—not just in terms of future potential, but with concrete demos from SAP and partners. Attendees got a glimpse of how AI can support their everyday work. This theme also shaped a panel discussion on how AI will influence future EHS and Product Compliance processes.

Another highlight was the Discovery Hub. Attendees had the chance to get hands-on experience with the solutions, following trial tours through dangerous goods and compliance document management processes in the Product Compliance room, as well as AI-assisted compliance management and AI-powered Safety Instruction Generation for Risk Assessment in the EHS room. Our SAP experts were kept very busy supporting visitors and discussing scenarios in depth.

Whether during breaks in the exhibition hall at partner or SAP booths, at the evening BBQ, or in the interactive sessions throughout the days—there was always something happening. Peers had countless opportunities to exchange ideas on topics affecting their day-to-day work. Despite the professional focus, the atmosphere remained friendly, honest and open. I’m already looking forward to seeing everyone again!

And that brings me to what’s coming next. Please save the dates:

  • SAP EHS & Product Compliance Virtual Infodays
    September 29 – October 1, 2026
    More information and invitations will follow soon.
  • SAP EHS & Product Compliance Conference presented by TAC Insights
    March 23–24, 2027 in Berlin, Germany
    Visit the event page for details.

 If you’d like to hear more or are interested in contributing to any of the above, feel free to contact us at product.compliance@sap.com.

entry.title: In current Onboarding the Applicant status is changed to started regardless of actual start date
entry.content:
Currently, in onboarding process, the Applicant Status is changed to "Started" even when hire date is in future. The functionality to introduce an intermediate "Hired" status that prevents automatic transition to "Started" before the actual start date is not available as a standard feature in SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding. The existing system is designed to automatically update candidate status to "Started" upon onboarding task completion, regardless of the Actual Start Date, which creates the data inconsistency. 

I have raised below enhancement request to introduce an Intermediate step to capture the correct Applicant status. Request you to kindly vote if you think this enhancement will be useful.

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/367458

Thank you.

Regards,

Amrita

entry.title: Re: Inflation Index linked Bonds (Localization for Treasury Brazil)
entry.content:
Hello Elaine,

Thanks for your guide.

I have a question regarding amortization in this type of bond.

PMP is customized with amortization step.

When i create a purchase system will generate premium/discount automatically based on the difference between my Purchase Price X FWZZ (Field Nominal Amount).

In Brazil, this is not my premium/discount. Premium/discount is the difference  between my Purchase Price X Market Price (FW17). 

I've tested several options in customizing but it doesnt work, all the settings lead to Nominal amount in FWZZ again.

To be able to "fix" this i could create several Class IDs (FWZZ) with different Nominal Amounts, however this is not a "clean" solution. Some customers buy dozens of Bonds every day, so i should create several IDs, which is not correct. The other option could be the Badi for Manipulating flows, but since im on a Public Cloud this is not possible.
Could you please guide over this topic?

entry.title: Accessibility - Focus visible & CSS
entry.content:
After an accessibility audit on SAP SuccessFactors, here are some non-compliances that were noted regarding Accessibility - CSS and Visible Focus that we would appreciate to see evolve.

Visible focus on fields:
Currently, the focus on certain fields is not sufficiently visible.
Recommendation: Maintain a minimum contrast of 3:1 for the focus to comply with WCAG accessibility standards.

Readability when CSS is disabled:
Some elements become unreadable if CSS is disabled.
Ensure all information remains readable even without CSS styling.

Please address these issues or advise us on recommended actions to resolve them.

entry.title: Re: Asset Accounting
entry.content:
The requirement is to just handle a change in the COA and push that down to the asset records?  Just change the account determination in config and do a JE in the GL to move the balances.  There's no way you should be mass transferring the entire subledger just for a change in the COA. 

Secondly, even if I was to do it your way... and to be clear, I wouldn't touch this with a ten foot pole because of the many calculation issues that might come up for records that you have no idea what has been done to them... I'd just post them as native post-caps, not do a legacy conversion.

entry.title: Re: Issue in Milestone Billing in CPM Project
entry.content:
What you are experiencing is standard system behavior. Once a billing milestone is fully consumed (100 EUR in your case), the milestone is marked as “billed” and cannot be reopened or reduced automatically when a credit note is issued later. The credit note adjusts the financials, but it does not reset the milestone consumption.

Options to handle the scenario

1) Create a new milestone for the adjustment
Add a new milestone in the project for the returned goods (e.g., “Credit Adjustment – 50 EUR”). This allows you to track the reversal explicitly in CPM.

2) Manual adjustment in project billing plan
Adjust the billing plan by adding a negative milestone or reducing a future milestone by the credit amount. This keeps the project’s billing plan aligned with financial postings.

3) Use credit memo functionality only
Accept that the milestone remains fully billed, but rely on the credit memo to balance revenue. This is the simplest approach but may cause reporting mismatches in project billing vs. FI.

entry.title: Accessibility - The scripts
entry.content:
Following an accessibility audit of SAP SuccessFactors, here are some non-conformities that were identified regarding scripts, and for which we would like to see improvements:

to ensure accessibility tools work effectively on this interface

here is a recommendation for scripts

  • Role = Menu; ​
  • State = Selected (aria-selected) + collapsed/expanded (aria-expanded); ​
  • Name = Select a language.

This will be implemented in the Menu component’s code. The elements should have a role = Menu with an ARIA-EXPANDED attribute set to true on the current button when it is expanded and false otherwise.

entry.title: Re: which program to run to update KSSK table
entry.content:
When you create a post and expecting some assistance from this forum, please share as much information as possible.  Just one line as you posted, will be a challenge to assist you.

KSSK is the classification assignment table, and it is updated by standard classification transactions (like CL20N, CL24N) or by master data maintenance transactions when classification is integrated (e.g. MM01/MM02 for materials, XD02 for customers). There is not a single “program” you run to update it as it depends on the object type and process.
Could you clarify which object type you are working with (e.g. material, customer, vendor)? Also, are you looking for a manual transaction to update classification, or do you need a batch program/mass update solution? That will help me point you to the right tool or program.

entry.title: VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon 2026: Champions Crowned at SYNC DemoJam
entry.content:
The Stage is Set
The VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon 2026 reached its spectacular grand finale on the main stage of VNSG SYNC 2026 – the annual VNSG and SAP Netherlands year event. In front of a packed keynote audience on 12 March, three finalist teams had exactly five minutes to demonstrate the business impact of their SAP BTP-powered innovations. The audience then cast their votes – and the results were electric.

From Challenge to Champion: The Journey
The hackathon launched in late 2025 when VNSG, SAP BTP Product Management and SAP NL Presales invited Dutch SAP customers and partners to submit real-life business challenges. Six teams were selected and, after a structured enablement phase, spent weeks co-building solutions with dedicated coaches. In February 2026, all six teams pitched to a jury panel, which selected three finalists for the grand DemoJam finale at SYNC. The three finalists stood out for their clear business relevance, technical ambition and the real-world potential to take their prototypes to production.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Champion: Promocean + SOA People — Purchase Order Control Center
Luc van Elk and Thomas Langhout from Promocean, supported by SOA People, took the audience vote and claimed the top spot. Their solution – the Purchase Order Control Center – puts SAP Business AI at the heart of procurement operations.
By connecting AI directly to SAP ECC, the team demonstrated how intelligent automation can dramatically reduce manual effort, accelerate purchase order processing, and surface exceptions before they become costly problems. A showcase of how AI on SAP BTP can deliver operational excellence in one of the most common – and pain-prone – business processes in any enterprise.

Runner-up: Ecotone + Expertum — Integration Suite Management
Arjan de Mol and Bart van de Kamp from Ecotone, partnered with Expertum Consulting, earned second place with a solution that tackles a pain many integration teams know all too well: managing and controlling large numbers of iFlows across landscapes. Their Integration Suite Management solution brings Joule-powered automation to iFlow lifecycle management – enabling operators to undeploy and deploy groups of iFlows intelligently. This turns a time-consuming, error-prone manual process into a governed, AI-assisted workflow, giving integration teams transparency, stability and control. 

Third Place: NTT DATA Business Solutions — ProTrack
Laurens Steffers and Jorrit van der Sanden from NTT DATA Business Solutions presented ProTrack – a solution delivering real-time insight for machines on the production floor. Built on SAP BTP, ProTrack addresses a critical operational need in manufacturing: knowing what is happening on your shop floor right now, not minutes later. By streaming machine data into an SAP BTP-powered dashboard, teams can react faster to exceptions, reduce downtime and optimize throughput.

 

Beyond the Podium: A Strong Field
The jury had a hard job. Aarini Consulting, Nederlandse Spoorwegen (NS), and Simac IT & BSC also built
impressive solutions that demonstrated real business value on SAP BTP. Every team that reached the jury pitch proved
that you can go from business challenge to working prototype in a short time - when you combine the right platform
with the right people. A sincere congratulations to all six participating teams !!

Behind the Scenes
Want to experience the atmosphere of the DemoJam from backstage? Watch the behind-the-scenes video - including a
live demo of how SAP Business AI is changing the way people interact with SAP, presented by Jason Enti and Ronald
Schippers: Behind the scenes at the VNSG SAP BTP DemoJam SYNC 2026

The People Who Made it Happen
A hackathon like this does not happen by itself. A huge thank you to everyone who contributed:
Presented at SYNC by: Twan van den Broek and Irene Rompa
Team coaches: Jan Penninkhof , Alex van den Hoek and Twan van den Broek
Jury: Ronald Schippersm Steven Nuyts, Steffen Schad, Jonathan Freeland
Made possible by: Mientje Paais, Olaf Winkler, Judy Bui and Niklas Siemer

What This Proves
The VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon 2026 is proof that innovation does not require a blank slate or a greenfield project. It requires the right platform, the right people and a real problem worth solving.
SAP BTP – combining SAP Integration Suite, SAP Build, SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Business AI and cloud-native runtimes – gives teams everything they need to move from prototype to production at speed.
The finalist solutions were not demos for the sake of demos. They addressed real procurement pain, real integration
complexity and real manufacturing visibility gaps. And they were built in weeks, not months.

What's Next?
The conversation does not end on the SYNC stage. All teams are encouraged to work with their coaches to plan production rollouts and turn hackathon prototypes into enterprise solutions.

For the broader SAP Community : Stay tuned for the next edition of the VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon. Bring your own
real-life business challenge and co-build with partners and SAP experts.
And who knows - your solution could be on the SYNC main stage in 2027.

SAP Business Technology Platform SAP Build SAP Integration Suite SAP Business AI 

@hansvp @qmrjvd @sricsi98 @BvE @mdschoenmakers @tamasszirtes @f_van_leeuwen @Petra1 @BartvdKamp @tedcastelijns @Arjan_deMol @dvvelzen @JdTeuling @AnnikaHeus @LaurensSteffers

Related reading:
When Innovation Meets Real Business Impact – VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon 2026
Grand Finale VNSG SAP BTP Hackathon in a DemoJam

entry.title: Building the Future of Enterprise AI: MCP Servers and Agent-to-Agent Communication Revolution
entry.content:
Building the Future of Enterprise AI: MCP Servers and Agent-to-Agent Communication Revolution

Transforming how AI systems collaborate in modern enterprise environments

 

The enterprise AI landscape is experiencing a paradigm shift. While individual AI agents have proven their worth in specific domains, the real transformation happens when these intelligent systems can seamlessly collaborate, share knowledge, and coordinate complex workflows. Enter two revolutionary protocols that are reshaping how we think about AI integration: Model Context Protocol (MCP) and Agent-to-Agent Protocol (A2A).

The Integration Challenge: Breaking Down AI Silos

Picture this: You're managing an e-commerce platform with specialized AI agents for inventory management, fraud detection, customer recommendations, and order fulfillment. Each agent performs exceptionally within its domain, but when a customer wants to modify a large order that was flagged for unusual purchasing patterns while using a promotional code—suddenly your streamlined system becomes a coordination nightmare.

This scenario illustrates the fundamental challenge facing modern enterprises: AI agents operating in isolation. Without standardized communication protocols, organizations struggle with:

  • Integration Complexity: Each new AI tool requires custom integration work, consuming valuable development resources
  • Data Inconsistency: Different systems use varying data formats, leading to translation errors and processing delays
  • Workflow Fragmentation: Multi-step processes require manual intervention to bridge gaps between AI systems
  • Scalability Limitations: Adding new capabilities means exponentially more integration points to maintain

The result? Expensive, brittle AI implementations that fail to deliver their full potential.

Model Context Protocol (MCP): The Universal AI Language

 

Model Context Protocol addresses the first half of this challenge by creating a standardized way for AI applications to communicate with external systems and data sources. Developed by Anthropic, MCP serves as a universal translator that eliminates the need for custom integrations.

How MCP Transforms System Integration

Traditional AI integration requires developers to:

  • Learn each system's unique API structure
  • Handle different authentication methods
  • Parse varying data formats
  • Maintain separate codebases for each integration

MCP changes this by providing a unified interface that abstracts away system-specific complexities. Think of it as having a universal remote control that works with every device in your smart home, regardless of brand or model.

Core MCP Components

MCP Host: Acts as the central coordination hub, managing all communication between AI clients and external systems.

MCP Client: Represents your AI application that needs to access external resources or capabilities.

MCP Server: Exposes specific functionalities, data sources, or services in a standardized format that any MCP client can understand.

What MCP Servers Provide

Tools: Executable functions that AI agents can discover and use dynamically. For example, a customer service AI could discover and invoke tools for order lookup, refund processing, or inventory checking without needing pre-programmed knowledge of each system.

Resources: Access to both static and dynamic data sources. This might include real-time inventory levels, customer databases, product catalogs, or analytical reports—all accessible through a consistent interface.

Prompts: Reusable, parameterized templates that maintain consistency across different AI interactions while allowing for customization based on specific contexts.

Real-World MCP Benefits: E-commerce Transformation

Consider an online retailer implementing MCP across their AI ecosystem:

Before MCP: Their recommendation engine needed separate integrations with the inventory system, customer database, pricing engine, and analytics platform. Each integration required months of development and ongoing maintenance.

After MCP: The recommendation engine simply queries the MCP ecosystem for "customer profile," "available products," and "pricing data." The MCP servers handle all the complexity of connecting to underlying systems, transforming data formats, and managing authentication.

Result: Development time reduced from months to weeks, maintenance overhead decreased by 60%, and new AI capabilities can be added without touching existing integrations.

MCP's Business Impact

Accelerated Development: Teams can focus on AI logic rather than integration plumbing, reducing time-to-market for new AI features by up to 70%.

Enhanced Reliability: Standardized interfaces reduce integration bugs and improve system stability.

Future-Proofing: When underlying systems change, only the MCP server needs updating—all connected AI clients continue working without modification.

Cost Efficiency: Reduced integration complexity means lower development and maintenance costs, with some organizations reporting 50% savings on AI infrastructure expenses.

Agent-to-Agent Protocol (A2A): Orchestrating AI Collaboration

 

While MCP solves the AI-to-system challenge, Agent-to-Agent Protocol tackles the equally important problem of AI-to-AI communication. A2A enables different AI agents to discover, communicate, and coordinate with each other to handle complex, multi-step workflows.

The Multi-Agent Coordination Challenge

Modern business processes rarely fit within a single AI agent's capabilities. Consider a customer support scenario where a client reports a billing error on their account. This seemingly simple request actually requires coordination between:

  • Customer Service AI: To understand the query and manage the interaction
  • Billing System AI: To identify and analyze the billing discrepancy
  • Fraud Detection AI: To ensure the request isn't part of a fraudulent pattern
  • Account Management AI: To determine appropriate remediation steps

Without A2A, each handoff between agents requires manual intervention or custom integration work.

A2A's Coordination Framework

Dynamic Discovery: Agents can automatically discover other agents' capabilities through standardized "Agent Cards" that describe what each agent can do, what inputs it needs, and what outputs it provides.

Role-Based Organization: A2A defines clear roles—coordinators who manage workflows, specialists who handle specific tasks, and observers who monitor and provide insights.

Task Lifecycle Management: Every interaction follows a structured lifecycle from task creation through completion, with standardized status updates and error handling.

Flexible Communication: Agents can exchange various content types—text, images, structured data, or complex multimedia—through a unified messaging framework.

Transformative Healthcare Use Case

Let's explore how A2A revolutionizes healthcare coordination—a domain where precise, timely collaboration literally saves lives:

Scenario: A patient arrives at the emergency department with chest pain.

Traditional Approach:

  • Triage nurse manually enters patient data into multiple systems
  • Various specialists are paged individually
  • Lab orders are placed through separate systems
  • Results are manually communicated between departments
  • Treatment decisions involve multiple phone calls and consultations

A2A-Enabled Approach: The Emergency Coordinator Agent automatically orchestrates the entire process:

  1. Immediate Assessment: Delegates initial evaluation to the Triage AI, which processes symptoms and assigns priority
  2. Parallel Specialist Coordination: Simultaneously engages Cardiology AI for cardiac risk assessment, Lab AI for stat blood work orders, and Radiology AI for imaging protocols
  3. Real-Time Collaboration: As results become available, each specialist AI provides recommendations that are automatically integrated into a comprehensive assessment
  4. Coordinated Decision Making: The Treatment Planning AI synthesizes all inputs to recommend optimal care pathways
  5. Resource Optimization: Bed Management AI ensures appropriate room assignment while Pharmacy AI prepares necessary medications

Outcome: What traditionally took hours of coordination happens in minutes, with improved accuracy and reduced human error.

A2A's Healthcare Benefits

Reduced Response Times: Emergency protocols that previously required 30-45 minutes now complete in under 10 minutes.

Improved Accuracy: Standardized communication eliminates transcription errors and ensures all relevant information is considered.

Enhanced Care Quality: Parallel processing allows for more comprehensive evaluation without delays.

Resource Optimization: Intelligent coordination prevents resource conflicts and optimizes staff utilization.

Audit Trail: Every decision and communication is logged, providing complete visibility into care processes.

The Synergy: MCP + A2A = Enterprise AI Excellence

The true power emerges when MCP and A2A work together, creating an ecosystem where AI agents can seamlessly access any enterprise system while coordinating sophisticated workflows with other agents.

Manufacturing Excellence Example

Consider a smart manufacturing facility where quality control, predictive maintenance, supply chain, and production planning agents work together:

MCP Integration: Each agent accesses manufacturing data through standardized interfaces—production metrics, equipment sensors, inventory levels, and quality reports—without needing to understand the underlying industrial systems.

A2A Coordination: When the predictive maintenance agent detects potential equipment failure, it automatically:

  • Alerts the production planning agent to adjust schedules
  • Informs the supply chain agent to expedite replacement parts
  • Notifies the quality control agent to increase inspection frequency
  • Coordinates with the facility management agent to schedule maintenance windows

Result: Unplanned downtime reduced by 80%, maintenance costs decreased by 40%, and product quality improved through proactive interventions.

Financial Services Transformation

In banking, compliance, risk management, customer service, and fraud detection agents collaborate to provide comprehensive financial services:

Real-Time Risk Assessment: When a customer applies for a loan, the application automatically triggers parallel processing across multiple AI agents—credit analysis, fraud detection, regulatory compliance, and market risk assessment—with results synthesized in minutes rather than days.

Automated Compliance: Regulatory changes automatically propagate through the agent network, with each agent updating its decision-making criteria and reporting mechanisms without human intervention.

Enhanced Customer Experience: Customer service agents can instantly access comprehensive customer profiles while coordinating with specialized agents for investment advice, loan modifications, or dispute resolution.

Strategic Implementation Benefits

Organizational Advantages

Scalability Without Complexity: Adding new AI capabilities becomes as simple as plugging in new agents—the underlying infrastructure handles all communication and coordination automatically.

Reduced Technical Debt: Standardized protocols eliminate the accumulation of custom integrations that become expensive to maintain over time.

Faster Innovation: Teams can experiment with new AI agents without worrying about integration challenges, accelerating the pace of innovation.

Improved ROI: Organizations typically see 3x faster deployment of new AI capabilities and 60% reduction in integration costs.

Operational Excellence

Enhanced Reliability: Built-in error handling, circuit breakers, and failover mechanisms ensure system resilience even when individual components fail.

Better Visibility: Comprehensive monitoring and logging provide unprecedented insight into AI system performance and decision-making processes.

Simplified Management: Centralized configuration and monitoring reduce operational complexity while improving control over AI behaviors.

Compliance Readiness: Standardized audit trails and governance frameworks make it easier to meet regulatory requirements.

Addressing Implementation Challenges

Security and Trust Considerations

Authentication and Authorization: Both protocols include robust security frameworks to ensure only authorized agents can access specific resources or communicate with other agents.

Data Privacy: Built-in privacy controls protect sensitive information while enabling necessary collaboration between agents.

Audit and Compliance: Comprehensive logging and monitoring capabilities support regulatory requirements and internal governance policies.

Performance Optimization

Efficient Communication: Both protocols are designed for high-performance scenarios with minimal overhead and optimized message formats.

Load Balancing: Intelligent distribution of work across agent networks prevents bottlenecks and ensures optimal resource utilization.

Caching and Optimization: Smart caching mechanisms reduce redundant operations and improve overall system responsiveness.

Change Management

Gradual Adoption: Organizations can implement MCP and A2A incrementally, starting with high-value use cases and expanding over time.

Training and Education: Success requires investment in team education to help developers and administrators understand the new paradigms.

Cultural Shift: Moving from custom integrations to standardized protocols requires organizational commitment to new development approaches.

Future Outlook: The Evolution of AI Collaboration

Emerging Trends

Multi-Modal Communication: Next-generation implementations will support rich media communications, enabling agents to share images, video, audio, and complex data visualizations as part of their coordination efforts.

Federated Learning: Agents will be able to share and improve their models collaboratively while maintaining data privacy and security.

Self-Healing Networks: Advanced implementations will automatically detect and recover from failures, redistributing work and spawning replacement agents as needed.

Cross-Organization Collaboration: Future versions may enable secure collaboration between agents across organizational boundaries, creating industry-wide AI ecosystems.

Industry-Specific Evolution

Healthcare: Integration with medical devices, electronic health records, and regulatory systems will enable comprehensive patient care coordination.

Financial Services: Real-time market data integration and regulatory compliance automation will transform trading, lending, and risk management.

Manufacturing: Integration with IoT sensors, robotics, and supply chain systems will enable fully autonomous production optimization.

Retail: Seamless integration across online and offline channels will create truly omnichannel customer experiences.

Measuring Success: Key Performance Indicators

Technical Metrics

Integration Speed: Time to integrate new AI capabilities reduced from months to weeks

System Reliability: Uptime improvements of 99.9% or higher through robust error handling

Performance Optimization: Response time improvements of 40-60% through efficient communication protocols

Maintenance Reduction: 50-70% reduction in integration maintenance overhead

Business Metrics

Time to Market: Accelerated deployment of new AI features and capabilities

Cost Savings: Reduced development and maintenance costs through standardization

Innovation Velocity: Increased rate of AI experimentation and deployment

Competitive Advantage: Enhanced ability to respond quickly to market changes and opportunities

Getting Started: Practical Implementation Steps

Phase 1: Assessment and Planning

Current State Analysis: Evaluate existing AI implementations and identify integration pain points

Use Case Prioritization: Select high-value scenarios where MCP and A2A can deliver immediate benefits

Technical Readiness: Assess infrastructure and team capabilities for protocol implementation

Phase 2: Pilot Implementation

Limited Scope Deployment: Start with a single, well-defined use case to prove value and learn lessons

Performance Baseline: Establish metrics for comparing before and after implementation results

Team Training: Invest in education and skill development for development and operations teams

Phase 3: Scaled Deployment

Gradual Expansion: Systematically extend implementation to additional use cases and systems

Governance Framework: Establish policies and procedures for managing the expanded AI ecosystem

Continuous Optimization: Regular performance review and optimization of agent interactions and system performance

Conclusion: The Collaborative AI Future

The integration of Model Context Protocol and Agent-to-Agent Protocol represents a fundamental shift in how we architect and deploy enterprise AI systems. By standardizing communication between AI and enterprise systems (MCP) and enabling sophisticated coordination between AI agents (A2A), organizations can unlock the full potential of collaborative artificial intelligence.

The transformation is profound:

  • Development cycles shortened from months to weeks
  • Maintenance overhead reduced by 50-70%
  • System reliability improved to 99.9%+ uptime
  • Innovation velocity increased by 3x or more

The opportunity is immediate:

  • Protocols are mature and ready for enterprise deployment
  • Implementation can begin with high-value, low-risk pilot projects
  • Benefits compound as more agents and systems join the ecosystem

The competitive advantage is significant:

  • Organizations that adopt these protocols will be able to deploy AI capabilities faster and more reliably than competitors
  • The ability to coordinate complex, multi-agent workflows will become a key differentiator
  • Standardized approaches will enable innovation at unprecedented scale

Whether you're in healthcare, finance, manufacturing, or retail, MCP and A2A provide the foundation for building sophisticated, resilient, and scalable AI ecosystems that can transform how your organization operates and competes.

The future of enterprise AI isn't about individual agents working in isolation—it's about orchestrated networks of specialized agents working together seamlessly to deliver exceptional business outcomes. Start your journey toward collaborative AI excellence today.

Key Resources:

  • Strategic Planning: Begin with a comprehensive assessment of your current AI landscape and identify high-value integration opportunities
  • Technical Implementation: Consider partnering with experienced system integrators who understand both protocols
  • Change Management: Invest in team education and organizational change management to ensure successful adoption
  • Continuous Improvement: Plan for ongoing optimization and expansion of your collaborative AI ecosystem

entry.title: Re: BAPI_MATPHYSINV_COUNT Document 100010396 does not exi...
entry.content:
This reply is a bit late, but for other folks having this same issue, the solution for me was:

- Call BAPI BAPI_MATPHYSINV_CREATE_MULT and get the document number from the return table (field MESSAGE_V1)
- Call the FM CONVERSION_EXIT_ALPHA_INPUT and pass the document number
- Select from IKPF using the document number and get the GJAHR (fiscal year)
- Use the GJAHR from IKPF as the fiscal year when calling the BAPI BAPI_MATPHYSINV_COUNT

entry.title: Re: Scope added but nodes are not there for aATP
entry.content:
Dear @tushar_sonawane53,

In Public Cloud adding 1JW / 6N0 in CBC does not mean you will automatically see a classic on-premise SPRO-style IMG subtree for aATP. In Public Cloud SAP exposes this area through CBC configuration activities and dedicated Fiori apps. SAP explicitly states that the availability of configuration activities depends on the selected scope, including scenarios and countries/regions and that these activities are handled in CBC. SAP also documents that configuration activities are available in a hierarchical business-area structure but they can and should also be searched directly by name, keyword or ID

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/doc/c38e987da8cf471f8888d703938f2ef1/SHIP3.0/en-US/SAPCentralBusinessConfiguration_ProjectExperience_en-US.pdf

Steps to check:

1. SAP states that scope and organizational structure must be deployed to the target system and configuration is then performed in the product-specific configuration phase. If the scope was only added in CBC but not properly deployed the related activities will not be available in the target configuration context

2. SAP documents this as the place where business-process configuration is performed in Public Cloud and also states that you can search by name, keyword or ID

 

3. SAP explicitly notes that when you launch a configuration activity from CBC, it opens the target system and the user must also have the corresponding authorizations in that target system. Missing target roles can make the activities/apps appear unavailable even if the scope is correct

4. SAP Public Cloud documentation for aATP shows that core functionality is split across configuration topics and dedicated apps for example:

  • Availability Checks / Product Availability Check with topics such as Checking Rules, Scheduling of Dates and Defining the Product Availability Check
  • Backorder Processing (BOP) with apps such as Configure BOP Segment, Configure Custom BOP Sorting and Configure BOP Variant
  • Product Allocation (PAL) with apps such as Manage Product Allocation Planning Data and Manage Product Allocation Sequences
  • Supply Protection with the Manage Supply Protection app

 

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/eb735e0254f34254b549d380434b1e0c.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/10e9ab50bc27406296b0b78eff3f9fba.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/741d645473c50d4ee10000000a423f68.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/2a7db74efff042beae7185a6ddf8f9d7.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/cfc8aa5747c46c10e10000000a441470.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/9a62c143add0423c8567d6e105f9fe7d.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/fdbf5eabf0e84621a15c6965b3e01649.html

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Difference in VAT Tax – SD vs. FI
entry.content:
The issue concerns different VAT amounts on the invoice and the accounting document resulting from different calculation methods. The invoice is issued in the currency EUR, but the VAT is in the local currency PLN.

Invoice:

  1. VAT is first calculated for each line item in EUR.
  2. Rounding differences of conditions are summed and added to the item with the highest VAT amount.
  3. Then the total amount of the line items including the rounding difference, i.e. 8,722.88 EUR, is converted using the exchange rate 4.2645 PLN.
  4. The VAT in PLN amounts to 37,198.72.

     

Accounting Document:

  1. The calculated VAT in EUR, identical to the invoice, is converted for each line item into PLN using the exchange rate 4.2645 and rounded to two decimal places.
  2. Then all line items in PLN are summed, resulting in 37,201.30 PLN.
  3. The difference compared to the invoice (2.58 PLN) arises because when calculating VAT in PLN for each line item separately, there is no rounding of condition differences for this currency.

entry.title: Re: Restrict all users from a tcode
entry.content:
If you want to restrict transaction DHCDCMON for all users except one, you don’t need anything complex. You just need to control the authorization object(s) behind that transaction.

  • Remove the authorization for DHCDCMON from all general roles.
  • Create a dedicated role that contains the authorization for DHCDCMON.
  • Assign that role only to the one user who should retain access.

This way, only the designated user can execute DHCDCMON, while everyone else is restricted.

entry.title: Re: SAP/CCM - CCS USAGE = 5 Settlement condition with acc...
entry.content:
Hi @1214, 

Please review the configuration guide document for 2023 provided by SAP in the KBA: 
https://me.sap.com/notes/2535889/E

Based on this document, you can see that in your case you could use the condition type RES6 for your contract and add REA6 condition type to your billing document pricing schema. 

RES6: (extract from document)

Condition type RES6 Rebate has the same parameter values as RES1. You use this condition type in a condition contract for usual sales rebate scenarios to maintain sales rebate conditions and corresponding accruals in one condition record.


REA6: (extract from document)

You use this condition type in the pricing procedure of transactional documents (billing documents) to control the accruals posting for sales rebate condition RES6. Therefore, note that the Accruals checkbox is selected in the Control Data 2 group box and that RES6 is maintained as reference condition type. This condition type uses the delivered access sequence REA1 Sales Rebate Accruals. See also chapter Specify CC-Relevance for Condition Types to check the special settings for the Usage parameter for condition types RES6 and REA6.

 

Regards,
Gauthier VICTOR

entry.title: Re: IT0105 - Subtype 10 was delimit
entry.content:
Hi, 

Infotype 105 (Communication) is typically not delimited upon employee termination, with the following exceptions:

  1. BADI Implementation – A custom Business Add-In (BADI) has been configured to manipulate infotype data during the termination process.

  2. Primary Email Address Change – The employee's primary email address in SuccessFactors Employee Central (SF EC) has been modified subsequent to termination initiation.

In the absence of these conditions, infotype 105 records remain active and undelimited in the personnel file.

Best Regards

Giorgio

entry.title: How does SAP distinguish between Internal /External documents for Digital Access licensing
entry.content:
Hi Team 

We are looking for answers to these questions.

How does SAP technically distinguish between Internal and External documents for the purpose of licensing?
If a document is triggered by a licensed SAP application like Concur, is it 100% exempt from Digital Access charges?
To what extent can we use Technical User Segregation as a primary classification method if the SAP Passport is technically unfeasible for certain flows?
Should we split the technical users into two separate accounts—one for Internal and one for External—to create a cleaner audit trail?
What specific technical reports or tables serve as the "proof of origin" that SAP auditors will officially accept?
If we identify misclassified documents during a self-audit, what is the formal process for "whitelisting" or excluding those internal flows from the billable count?
How can we verify in our current S/4HANA or ECC system if SAP Passport functionality is active and correctly tagging incoming messages?
What logic is used to classify a document as "External" / “Internal” if the SAP Passport is missing or unsupported by the source system?
If SAP Passport was not enabled in the past, what is the recommended retroactive method to classify those historical documents for audit purposes?
What is the overall audit experience by other clients?

Thanks

Nancy

entry.title: Events in CSA: Real-Time Monitoring and Alerting Made Simple
entry.content:
Exploring Configuration and Security Analysis in SAP Cloud ALM – IT Admin perspective 

By now, most of us have had the opportunity to explore the Configuration and Security Analysis (CSA) new Validation application. The new features are truly helpful, aren't they?

CSA has now extended its capabilities with a powerful new "Events" functionality, which enables real-time monitoring, alerting, and automated responses to system changes and security events. This enhancement takes your IT infrastructure management to the next level.

What Are Events in SAP CALM?

Events in SAP CALM represent significant occurrences within your IT infrastructure that warrant attention, action, or audit trails. These critical events can include:

  • Configuration changes across systems
  • Security policy violations or compliance deviations
  • Authentication and authorization modifications

Setting Up Events in CSA

Getting Started with Event Configuration

In the Configuration and Security Analysis Validation application, an administrator can navigate to Configuration and configure Events by navigating to individual checks of the chosen service type and editing the Events tab.

Defining Your Scope

In the Scope section, users can add the systems/services for which they want to be notified.

Note: Providing no scope means the entire scope of all services/systems corresponding to that service type is relevant for alerting.

Event Naming and Description

In the Events section, users have the flexibility to either:

  • Provide a new event name and description, or
  • Reuse the check name and check description

Event Actions: Multiple Notification Options

The Event Actions section offers various downstream actions, allowing you to customize how alerts are handled:

  • ✓ Create Alert
  • ✓ Send Email to
  • ✓ Send Chat Notification
  • ✓ Create Ticket
  • ✓ Start Operation Flow

Save as Variant

Users who want to preserve their configuration for future use can save as a variant, which will later be available in the dropdown menu for easy reuse.

After maintaing all the necessary settings, user must save the checks.

Real-World Examples

Example 1: Alert Creation

Scenario: User Adam chooses to Create Alert

  • Adam will see the alerts in the Alert Inbox of CSA Data Stores application
  • Adam can also view open alerts in the Data Stores Card view
  • Clicking on "Alerting" Tab in Data Store Application or the "Open alerts" number in Data Stores Card navigates to the Alert Inbox where all alerts relevant to the selected scope are visible
  • In the Validation application, open alerts can be viewed as Alerts Count on the Validation overview page

Example 2: Email Notifications

Scenario: User John prefers email-only notifications

  • John enables only the Send Email to option
  • He provides one or more recipients who should receive emails

Email Content Includes:

  • Service Name
  • Service Type
  • Service Description
  • Object Details
  • Reported At timestamp
  • SAP Cloud ALM Tenant information
  • Rating

Email Features:

  • Contains a table showing non-compliant configuration items
  • Displays Status, Landscape Object, Configuration Item, Value, and Valid From details
  • Includes two links:
    1. Context-specific navigation to the Alert Inbox
    2. Direct link to the checks result page for verification of non-compliant items

Example 3: Chat Notifications

By enabling Chat notification, users can receive alerts directly on their subscribed chat channel, ensuring instant awareness of critical events.

Important Note

For Administrators: ACL (Access Control List) must not be applicable when setting up events in the CSA application.

Refer Configuring Events 

Conclusion

The new Events functionality in CSA transforms how organizations monitor and respond to system changes and security incidents. Whether you prefer alerts, emails, chat notifications, or automated tickets, CSA's flexible event configuration ensures that critical information reaches the right people at the right time.

Start configuring your events today and experience enhanced real-time monitoring and compliance management!

entry.title: Meet Up - Recife 16 de Abril
entry.content:
A todos da comunidade SAP de Recife: vamos ter a oportunidade de nos reunirmos após o SAP CodeJam, 16 de abril, que faz parte doSAP CodeJam Roadshow 2026 - Brazil edition ! 

O encontro irá acontecer 16 de abril as 18:00 BRT, então junte-se a nós e faça parte do grupo! Você não precisa participar ou ter participadonos nossos eventos SAP CodeJam, esse encontro é para qualquer membro da SAP Community que seja curioso ou ativo - o nosso foco são os #developers.

Vamos aproveitar este tempo para falar das novidades do mundo SAP, dos projetos interessantes que já participarem e até mesmo o que tem de novidade sobre vocês mesmo!

Por favor, caso deseje participar, clique em "I will attend". Nós temos reservado uma mesa para 12 no "Moendo na Lage".

Nós estamos felizes com a oportunidade de conectar as pessoas e ter um momento de lazer! Obs: Cada um é responsável por pagar sua comida e bebida

Clique aqui e saiba mais sobre o SAP CodeJam Roadshow 2026 - Brazil edition .

entry.title: Re: Time Evaluation
entry.content:
Hi, 

Poor performance typically originates from custom functions and/or operations within the Time schema.

To accurately identify root causes, I recommend executing comprehensive system traces using SAP's diagnostic transactions ST12 (Performance Trace Analysis) and SAT (ABAP Runtime Analysis). Given the technical complexity of these transactions, bringing in our Basis team upfront for system resource analysis and optimization support would be a smart move.

Best Regards

Giorgio

entry.title: Inconsistencies with Defining RBSC for Composable Storefront
entry.content:
There are inconsistencies across SAP documentation on setting up the .npmrc for the RBSC for Composable Storefront.

Building the Composable Storefront From Libraries | SAP Help Portal contains the following recommendation for the RBSC

@Spartacus:registry=https://73554900100900004337.npmsrv.base.repositories.cloud.sap/
//73554900100900004337.npmsrv.base.repositories.cloud.sap/:_auth=<npmcredentialsfromrbsc>
always-auth=true

Add Applications to JavaScript Storefronts | SAP Help Portal provides the following recommendation, pointing to a CDN-based RBSC URL

spartacus=https://73554900100900004337.npmsrv.cdn.repositories.cloud.sap

Which one here is accurate? Should we be leveraging @Spartacus:registry, @Spartacus, or spartacus for the repository URL?

entry.title: Using cXML Envelopes in Integration Advisor
entry.content:
I am happy to announce that we have extended our envelope support to the cXML Type System. Our B2B Library of predefined content now includes the envelope structures for cXML as well. As a result, you can now combine cXML Envelope and cXML Message parts into one Message Implementation Guideline (MIG). Doing so, you can use the envelope fields at other places like in Mapping Guidelines (MAGs). In this blog post I want to give you an overview of the new offering.

cXML Envelopes in Type System Content

The Type System Content in Integration Advisor was extended to contain the cXML Envelopes as well (starting from version 1.2.044). In Message Overview you can simply search for Envelope to find the according structures. 

cXML defines three different envelope structures: the EnvelopeForRequests, the EnvelopeForResponses plus the EnvelopeForMessages. Note as a cXML specialty that the choice of the Envelope depends on the Message Type:

  • Use EnvelopeForRequests for so called cXML Request messages like OrderRequest or InvoiceRequest.
  • Use EnvelopeForResponses for so called cXML Response messages like DataResponse or OrderStatusSetupResponse.
  • Use EnvelopeForMessages for so called cXML Message messages like SupplierChangeMessage or ProductActivityMessage.

By the way, if you navigate to the Message placeholder node within the envelope structures, you will find the fitting message types (shown as allowed code values).

Refer above screenshot for a cXML EnvelopeForRequests structure. Please note that the envelope structure is independent from the specific message(s) used and only shows a placeholder for the message level.

Message Implementation Guidelines with Envelopes 

For your Message Implementation Guideline (MIG) you have now the new option to combine Envelope and Message parts into one merged structure. 

When creating a new MIG for the cXML Type System, you will find the step Envelopes as part of the Creation Wizard. Here you can decide if you want to use an envelope or not and select the specific envelope structure you want to use.

As a result, your MIG starts with the root node cXML and has the envelope parts (see blue box above) plus the specific message parts (see green box above) according to the Message Type you have selected for your MIG. Refer above screenshot for an exemplary cXML OrderRequest MIG which includes the EnvelopeForRequests (with nodes Header and Request).

Envelope Usage in Cloud Integration

Previously, your Integration Flow in Cloud Integration needed an additional flow step to remove the envelope parts from the cXML payload. You don’t need this flow step anymore if you use an envelope inside your cXML MIG.

Additionally, we implemented another small improvement: If your MIG does not include an Envelope, then the Integration Advisor preprocessing (preproc.xslt) automatically removes the envelope part from the payload. This applies to all Type Systems that offer envelopes. In this way, you can even skip this additional flow step for MIGs without envelope.

Further cXML Content Improvements in Integration Advisor

As we have updated our cXML content, we used the chance to add a few more improvements to it (in accordance with the cXML Reference Guide):

  • All date/time fields (based on Simple Type datetime.tz) and all time fields (based on Simple Type time.tz) refer to a new special Date Time Format required by cXML. In both cases, this is the standard ISO 8601 format, but with mandatory time zone offset and Z not being allowed (like 2026-04-01T11:11:11+02:00 for Date/Time or 11:11:11+02:00 for Time).
  • The common xml:lang attribute is now used in its standard form inside your MIG.
  • All fields based on Simple Type UnitOfMeasure come now with a reference to the codelist Rec 20 from UN/CEFACT. 

Conclusion

With this extension we support envelope structures for the cXML Type System in Integration Advisor. You can now include envelope nodes into your Message Implementation Guideline (MIG) definition and as result use these nodes inside your Mapping Guidelines (MAGs). 

Further reading

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/integration-advisor-overview-of-components-for-... 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/integration-content-advisor-create-a-customized...

entry.title: Re: Run Datasphere Task Chains from SAC Multi-Actions via External API Step
entry.content:
Hi @JoelleS

In response of the API to start task chain: https://<host name>/api/v1/datasphere/tasks/chains/<dsp space name>/run/<technical name of task chain>, there will be a "logId" field returned in json format, if the task chain starts successfully. This "logId" filed should be mapped to the "Job Id" (as the above configuration section has already illustrated), thus in the subsequent polling API, it will be used replacing placeholder: ${jobId}, so that the polling API will query the status of the exact task chain run that's initiated in current API step execution.

Best regards,

Manu

entry.title: STO Billing (JSTO) Not Picking Price from Stock / Goods Issue
entry.content:
We are facing an issue in Stock Transfer Order (STO) within the same company code process.
Process Flow:
STO created using ME21N with document type UD
Outbound Delivery created via VL10B
Post Goods Issue (PGI) completed
Billing document created using JSTO billing type
Issue:
While creating the STO invoice, the price is showing as zero in the billing document.
Expected Behavior:
The system should pick the valuation price / stock price based on the Goods Issue (PGI) value from the outbound delivery.
Current Workaround:
Maintaining condition records manually to derive pricing in billing
Clarification Required:
Is there any standard configuration or setting to make the billing document pick:
Price from material valuation (stock value) OR
Price from PGI accounting document / goods issue value

entry.title: Function with data from a variable linked to another one
entry.content:
Hi, I don't know where to post a question regarding Business Object. If this isn't the right place, please redirect me.

I'm trying to make a function / measure that sums the values of some operations based on the ID. This is my data sample: 

Transaction order numberSale operation typeOperation amount
1410
1205
2205
3205

"Transaction Order Number" is a unique identifier for every operation, "Sale operation Type" defines the type of the operation and "Operation amount" is self explanatory.

Basically I have to sum the Operation Amount for all the Transaction Order Number that contains the Sale operation type 41. In my case "Sale Operation type" can only be 20 or 41, so it shouldn't be so difficult. In this example the result of my variable should be 5.

I tried doing it in 2 different ways, but I end up with #MULTIVALUE in both of them.

1) = If ([Transaction order number] Where([Sale operation type] = 41) = [Transaction order number] Where([Sale operation type] = 20)) Then Sum([Operation amount]) Else 0

2) = Sum([Operation amount]) ForEach([Transaction order number]) Where(Count([Sale operation type]) > 1)

In 1) the idea is to check if I have the same Transaction order number for Sales operation type 41 and 20.
In 2) the idea is to sum the Operation amount if I find multiple Sale operation type for the same Transaction order Number

Is there a way to do this?

Thank you for your time

entry.title: SSAM 2410- Icons too small in SAP Service and Asset Manager mobile app (Sync & Side Menu)
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are currently using the SAP Service and Asset Manager mobile application 2410 (metadata) and have encountered a usability issue related to icon sizes.

Specifically:

  • The sync icon appears very small and is difficult for users to notice and tap.
  • The side menu (drawer) icon is also quite small, which impacts accessibility and overall user experience.

This is particularly challenging for field users who rely on quick and efficient navigation in real-world working conditions.

Details:

  • Application: SAP Service and Asset Manager 2410
  • SSAM Mobile Client Version: 2511 (standard and custom)
  • Metadata Version: 2410
  • Platform: ios
  • Device(s): iPad /iPhone

Questions:

  1. Is there any configuration or setting available to adjust icon sizes in the application?
  2. Are there any known SAP Notes addressing this issue?
  3. Has anyone implemented a workaround or customization to improve icon visibility?

We would appreciate any guidance, recommendations, or references.

Thank you in advance!
